architectureDecision.label: Mirror task.* canonical commit
architectureDecision.rationale: Planning publish uses commitCanonicalTaskStateEvents semantics: mutation does not durably succeed unless publish/push succeeds; repair via existing task-state tools.

readyReview.decision: On publish failure, mirror task canonical commit — no local-first pending planning queue in Phase 119.

technicalBreakdown: Hook upsert-phase-catalog-entry post-save to commitCanonicalPlanningEvents (same failure semantics as tasks).
Hook update-workspace-status and set-current-phase to publish workspace status events.
Add integration test: publish failure leaves store unchanged / returns stable error codes matching task path.

acceptanceCriteria: Mutations under git-event-log authority append planning events and push to workflow-cannon/task-state.
Publish failure mirrors task.* canonical commit: command fails with stable codes; no durable local-only planning drift.
Workspaces with sqlite-only canonical authority behave unchanged (no publish attempted).
Publish conflicts surface task-state-stale-version or planning-equivalent stable codes.
